title: Evangelion Real time strategy game
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Real-time strategy game             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Sun Feb 15, 2009 11:13 pm I'm not sure if I post this in the right place but would anyone like to see a Evangelion Real-time strategy (RTS) similar to Halo Wars and Aliens versus Predator: Extinction? I was playing the Halo Wars demo a while ago and I thought the idea of Evangelion done in the style of Age of Mythology or Star Wars: Empire at War would be a fun idea. I think the idea can work out well. What do you think?

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sun Feb 15, 2009 11:16 pm I'd play it. I like real-time strategy games as well as Evangelion. So together it should be pretty good.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Feb 16, 2009 12:53 am Would be kinda pointless unless you strayed -way- off the cannon.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Mon Feb 16, 2009 1:00 am Real Time Tactics might work, considering the emphasis on the Evangelion units. It could be a bit like Battle Tech, but with an emphasis on the "hero" unit Evas. But you can't really do a Real Time Strategy game with it because almost no other units other than Evangelions matter in the Eva universe, and there are no resources to worry about or bases to make-- everything the player needs in the canon Eva universe has already been made for them at the start. Unless you change the setting a lot you aren't going to be able to create something like Star Craft or War Craft out of it.

[]:             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Mon Feb 16, 2009 1:02 am I don't think it would work. There are no armies fighting and thus it doesn't lend itself to strategy. On one side there's a big monster (Angel), and on the other side there's another big monster (Eva) and a bunch of tanks and planes (Nerv) that are entirely incapable of doing anything to the other side's big monster. So in a given Evangelion scenario, there is only one unit on each side that is capable of fighting. That's not strategy, that's pro wrestling.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Feb 16, 2009 1:55 am I'd rather see a Fallout-like RPG with post-instrumentality rebuilding of civilization. Lots of conflict between various groups of the reborn over scarce resources in the inhospitible wasteland near GNR's various body parts.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Feb 16, 2009 1:55 am I am kind of surprised that no one's pimped out Eva enough to make some RTS, but Eva doesn't quite lend itself to be a RTS like SAWgunner said but give 'em time, there'll be one soon enough IMO

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Feb 16, 2009 9:26 am If you want to play Evangelion as an RTS, there's Super Robot Wars. Granted, it's got mecha in it other than Eva, but it's pretty close.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Mon Feb 16, 2009 11:38 am The only problem is that one would have to create other basic units for the angel's faction. Throw in the VTOL aircraft, JSSDF tanks and soldiers, and you have the foundation for the Nerv faction. As for engines, how about Supreme Commander's? This could actually have some potential (if you are willing to bend and go beyond the canon).

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Wed Feb 18, 2009 7:34 am The Eva Monkey wrote: If you want to play Evangelion as an RTS, there's Super Robot Wars. Granted, it's got mecha in it other than Eva, but it's pretty close.

[]:             Quote Post by Lleonard Pler » Sun Feb 22, 2009 11:54 am I'm not sure about RTS, but it'd be great an Evangelion game inspired by Valkyria Chronicles type of gameplay...

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Feb 22, 2009 4:04 pm Neon Genesis Evangelions 2 for the PS2 and PSP is already technically a real-time strategy game.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Tue Feb 24, 2009 9:20 am The Eva Monkey wrote: If you want to play Evangelion as an RTS, there's Super Robot Wars. Granted, it's got mecha in it other than Eva, but it's pretty close.
